extra.reason: Completed: documented root-level M-1..5 attention jumps and jump-back binds with correct tmux-session-picker path, collision policy, and conflict notes.
Completed: added clear-cache subcommand, optional install.sh --tmux-hooks hook installer, README hook snippets, and contract coverage for cache clear/hooks.

Completed: session rows now render idle duration badges from session_activity, stale sessions are dimmed/sorted below active non-attention sessions by TMUX_PICKER_STALE_MINS, and contract tests cover formatter/list behavior.
Completed: Alt-x pane kill remains immediate; session kill now uses tmux command-prompt defaulting to cancel, with kill-confirm helper, README update, and contract tests.
Completed: Ctrl-n create picker over known/configured worktrees plus zoxide/~/.z recents, deterministic collision-safe session names, create-session flow, README docs, and contract coverage.

Validation: bash -n scripts/bin/tests and make test (81 pass, 0 fail).

wait-agent shipped: resolves target to pane, polls @agent_state first with UI inference fallback, supports --timeout/--interval durations, exits when no longer working/running/busy/thinking, docs/README/tests updated. Validation: bash -n scripts/bin/tests and make test (87 pass, 0 fail).

Validation: bash -n scripts/bin/tests and make test (94 pass, 0 fail).

No code fix required — the picker already exposes the right primitive.

Discovery during E2E investigation (see comment):
- `monitor-agent`: registers a background daemon in the picker's monitor registry, returns exit 0 immediately. Correct for pi's harness (which consumes envelope streams) and for shadow-mode + audit history. Does NOT hit Claude Code's task-notification pipe because the foreground shell exits before the daemon transitions.
- `wait-agent`: blocks the foreground shell in a poll loop until target transitions out of a working state, then returns exit 0. IS the notification when spawned via Claude's Bash({run_in_background: true}).

Verified 2026-07-12 at 17:53 CEST: after sending pi a message, spawning `Bash({run_in_background: true, command: 'tmux-session-picker wait-agent xtmux:1.2 --timeout 30m --interval 30s'})` produced a real task-notification block the moment pi transitioned done. No polling required.

Documentation-only remediation:
- AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md note: when sending durable messages via message-send / safe-send-pointer to another agent's pane, spawn `wait-agent` alongside via run_in_background if you need to notify. Auto-monitor-on-send hook already handles the daemon-registry side.
- Extension code unchanged: pi-auto-monitor stays the same (their harness has native surface).
- No changes to picker or Bun runtime — both primitives already ship.

- xtmux-3xs.20 (monitor-agent --wait-for-transition) remains a separate semantic fix for the daemon registry side; independent from this bead's resolution.
- xtmux-3xs.21 (sender receipt primitive) unblocked.

Three-hook coordination + native Monitor tool. .xtrm/hooks/auto-monitor-on-send.mjs augmented to touch <target>_pending; new auto-monitor-consumed.mjs (Monitor|Bash prefilter) clears on wait-agent; new auto-monitor-drain-stop.mjs (Stop) blocks with exact Monitor(wait-agent) invocation.
